alder-select.name: Alder Select
alder-select.description: Consistent color and grain with minimal natural characteristics. Color ranges from warm golden-yellow to a medium tan-brown, leaning warm but not strongly red. Soft straight grain, even texture. Great for staining.

alder-natural.name: Alder Natural
alder-natural.description: Noticeably more character than Select grade. Visible pin knots and sound knots scattered across the face, mineral streaking, and color variation ranging from pale yellow to reddish brown. Staves may show contrasting heartwood and sapwood tones. Should look distinctly 'natural' — not clean or uniform like Select.

alder-premium-knotty.name: Alder Premium Knotty
alder-premium-knotty.description: Sound knots which may contain star cracks. Some color and grain variation with limited small mineral streaks. Pin knot clusters limited. Premium selection of allowable characteristics.

alder-rustic-knotty.name: Alder Rustic Knotty
alder-rustic-knotty.description: Even, consistent light warm-brown color with a honey-yellow base and mild reddish tint. Minimal color variation between staves. Typically one smaller knot around 1 inch (which may be open or contain cracks) plus a few scattered pin knots — NOT covered in large knots. Very little mineral streaking. Do not overdo the knots; a real door has character but is not overwhelmed by them.

cherry-select.name: Cherry Select
cherry-select.description: Minimized color variation. Reduced allowance for pin knots. May display small amounts of pitch pockets on the face.

cherry-natural.name: Cherry Natural
cherry-natural.description: Larger variation in natural characteristics. May contain pitch pockets, closed knots, pin knots, sapwood on face, and more color variation.

maple-select.name: Maple Select
maple-select.description: Least amount of color and grain variation. Lighter color tone, great for clear or light stain.

maple-natural.name: Maple Natural
maple-natural.description: Light color tone with allowance for mineral streaks, worm tracks, and varying amounts of heartwood and sapwood on the face. May have small closed knots or pin knots.

maple-paint-grade.name: Maple Paint Grade
maple-paint-grade.description: Lowest visual grade of maple. Moderate mineral streaks, green and grey discoloration, mismatched staves with visible color contrast between pieces. May have putty fills, pin knots, and worm tracks on face. Intended to be painted over — not attractive as a natural finish.

mdf-panel-maple-paint-grade-frame.name: MDF Panel / Maple Paint Grade Frame
mdf-panel-maple-paint-grade-frame.description: MDF panel paired with Maple Paint Grade Frame for painting purposes. IMPORTANT: The center panel must be smooth MDF (medium-density fiberboard) — perfectly smooth, flat, grainless surface with a uniform warm tan-brown color like pressed wood fiber. No wood grain on the panel. The frame (rails and stiles) should be Maple Paint Grade matching the swatch. The panel and frame must be visually distinct — smooth grainless tan MDF panel against light grained maple frame.

mdf-panel-soft-maple-frame.name: MDF Panel / Soft Maple Frame
mdf-panel-soft-maple-frame.description: MDF panel paired with Soft Maple Frame for painting purposes.

red-oak-select.name: Red Oak Select
red-oak-select.description: Color-sorted for uniformity — consistent wheat-golden to pinkish-brown tones across all staves, leaning more yellow than red. No wild swings between light and dark pieces. Open grain pattern with small linear pores. Limited mineral streaking. If it shows dramatic color variation, it's Natural, not Select.

red-oak-natural.name: Red Oak Natural
red-oak-natural.description: Wheat-pink to red color tones with allowance for color variation, mineral streaking, small closed knots, pin knots, and some sapwood.

red-oak-quarter-sawn.name: Red Oak Quarter Sawn
red-oak-quarter-sawn.description: MUST show prominent, highly visible medullary ray flake (tiger stripe pattern) — this is the defining feature. Flakes should be bold, frequent, and unmistakable across the face of every stave, not subtle or sparse. Without visible flakes it is NOT Quarter Sawn. Straight grain alone is Rift Sawn; arched grain is Plain Sawn. Wheat-golden to warm pinkish-tan color with a subtle red undertone. Well-defined, darker grain specks for contrast. Select grade characteristics with reduced allowance for color variation, mineral streaks, and knots.

red-oak-rift.name: Red Oak Rift
red-oak-rift.description: Straight vertical grain lines ONLY. NO medullary ray flake (that is Quarter Sawn) and NO arched cathedral grain (that is Plain Sawn). Tightest, most parallel lines of any Red Oak grade with well-defined, darker grain specks for contrast. Select grade characteristics with reduced allowance for color variation, mineral streaks, and knots. Wheat-golden to warm tan color, leaning more yellow than red.

white-oak-select.name: White Oak Select
white-oak-select.description: Straight grain pattern with coarse to medium-coarse texture. Small linear pores, ideal for staining. Reduced allowance for small closed knots, pin knots, color and grain variation. Some mineral streaks may be visible.

white-oak-natural.name: White Oak Natural
white-oak-natural.description: Coarse to medium-coarse texture with small linear pores, ideal for staining. Allowance for color and grain variation, some sap, small closed knots, and pin knots.

white-oak-rift.name: White Oak Rift
white-oak-rift.description: Straight vertical grain lines ONLY. NO medullary ray flake (that is Quarter Sawn) and NO arched cathedral grain (that is Plain Sawn). Tightest, most parallel lines of any White Oak grade. Minimal allowance for color variation, small closed knots, and pin knots.

white-oak-quarter-sawn.name: White Oak Quarter Sawn
white-oak-quarter-sawn.description: MUST show prominent, highly visible medullary ray flake (tiger stripe pattern) — this is the defining feature. Flakes should be bold, frequent, and unmistakable across the face of every stave, not subtle or sparse. Without visible flakes it is NOT Quarter Sawn. Straight grain alone is Rift Sawn; arched grain is Plain Sawn. Small allowance for color variation. Reduced allowance for pin knots, small closed knots, and mineral streaks.

european-steamed-beech.name: European Steamed Beech
european-steamed-beech.description: Relatively straight and even appearance, may contain chevron patterned grain. Consistent tan to reddish brown color from steaming process. Similar to Alder with small flecks in grain.

walnut-select.name: Walnut Select
walnut-select.description: Heartwood is light brown to dark chocolate brown with occasional purplish casts — NOT near-black. No sapwood on face. Grain pattern with straight and wavy sections. Reduced allowance for small knots and pin knots. Warm, saturated tones.

walnut-natural.name: Walnut Natural
walnut-natural.description: Heartwood is light brown to dark chocolate brown with occasional purplish casts — NOT near-black. Creamy white sapwood visible on face, noticeable mix of heartwood and sapwood. Grain pattern with straight, wavy, and additional pattern sections. Allowance for small knots, pin knots, mineral, and other natural characteristics.

soft-maple.name: Soft Maple
soft-maple.description: Distinctly yellow-cream to honey-toned — warmer and more golden than standard Maple. May have light pink and/or gray mixed in. Greater mineral streaking and generally has fleck in the grain.

white-birch-select.name: White Birch Select
white-birch-select.description: Cut from sapwood, consistent creamy white to light tan color with noticeable warm pinkish-red undertones — NOT pure white or cool-toned. Minimal color variation with reduced allowance for pin knots and mineral streaks.

natural-birch.name: Natural Birch
natural-birch.description: Cut from heartwood and sapwood with calico mix of Pink and White tones. May contain small closed knots, pin knots, mineral streaks, and color variation.

knotty-pine.name: Knotty Pine
knotty-pine.description: Yellowish white color. Medium-coarse texture with even grain. Allowance for larger closed knots, knots with star cracks, color variation, pitch pockets, and pin knots.

pecan-hickory-select.name: Pecan-Hickory Select
pecan-hickory-select.description: Heartwood is pale to reddish brown. Sapwood is white, tinged with fine brown lines. Frame (rails and stiles) should be heartwood. Calico color variation to blended look. Reduced allowance for small closed knots and pin knots. Some mineral streaking allowed.

pecan-hickory-natural.name: Pecan-Hickory Natural
pecan-hickory-natural.description: Stark variations between pale to reddish brown heartwood and white sapwood tinged with fine brown lines. Mineral streaking, small closed knots, and pin knots acceptable. Parts may be all heartwood or all sapwood.

pecan-hickory-rustic-knotty.name: Pecan-Hickory Rustic Knotty
pecan-hickory-rustic-knotty.description: Allows for mineral streaking, larger open knots, knots with star cracks, clusters of pin knots. Stark color variations between pale to reddish brown heartwood and white sapwood tinged with fine brown lines. Parts may be all heartwood or all sapwood.

sapele-ribbon-grain-mahogany.name: Sapele Ribbon Grain Mahogany
sapele-ribbon-grain-mahogany.description: Quarter Sawn Sapele with ribbon striped appearance. Color varies from golden brown to dark reddish brown. Color tends to darken with age.

ash-select.name: Ash Select
ash-select.description: Pale cream with straw-yellow undertones. Mild, subtle cathedral grain patterns with wide plain areas between streaks. Soft satin finish, bright and airy timber. Clean, understated organic texture, warm ivory wood. Open grain pattern with generous spacing. Some color variation and mineral allowed. Reduced allowance for small closed knots and pin knots.
